markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Fallback to passage retrieval when graph candidates are missing, stale, contradicted, or low-confidence. This MT belongs to RetrievalContextAndRanking. Retrieve project knowledge through explainable direct, graph, hybrid, and passage fallback modes.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Retrieval must choose explicit modes: none, direct_load, exact_lookup, graph_traversal, hybrid_rag, or passage_fallback, with non_hybrid_reason where applicable.
Context bundles must be bounded, cited, explainable, restartable, and inspectable through backend and visual-debug surfaces.
scope.expected_behavior: PassageFallbackPlanner leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: PassageFallbackPlanner is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified 4026ec85; RetrievalContextAndRanking: 46 unit + 10 real-PG tests pass; cheapest-authoritative planner records mode+skip-reason in replayable RetrievalTrace; reuses MT-060 trace/bundle substrate (1 new migration 0260); MT-129 additive QueryRetrievalMode extension; folded-stub bridges (139-142) preserve ProjectBrain/SemanticCatalog/AIReady/ContextPack intent as backend contracts.

evidence.finding: Passage fallback was a correct pure decision but unwired/unexecuted with no integration test
evidence.fix: New knowledge_retrieval::executor wires decide_passage_fallback into the executed retrieval path after graph traversal; on fallback the plan revises to PassageFallback (persisted hybrid_rag per MT-129 law) and the durable mode_reason + trace warnings/decisions record WHY
evidence.tests: knowledge_retrieval_pipeline_tests 12 passed incl. executed_pipeline_falls_back_to_passages_ranks_and_compiles (real-PG empty graph -> graph_candidates_missing -> ranked span-cited passage bundle persisted with replayable trace)

findings.title: passage fallback is a correct pure decision but is never wired/executed or integration-tested
findings.evidence: decide_passage_fallback (passage_fallback.rs:87) is only unit-tested; no planner/executor calls it and no PG/integration test exercises graph-result -> fallback -> passages. The compiler path (pipeline_tests:230) injects pre-built candidates, bypassing fallback.
findings.fix: Wire decide_passage_fallback into the retrieval execution path after graph traversal, and add a PG test where a missing/stale/contradicted graph drives a recorded passage-fallback trace.

claim_mismatches.claim: Fallback to passage retrieval when graph candidates are missing, stale, contradicted, or low-confidence
claim_mismatches.reality: the fallback DECISION is implemented and unit-proven, but no executed fallback path exists or is tested end-to-end

missing_tests: PG test: missing/stale/contradicted graph -> passage_fallback trace recorded with the right reason

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-133 requires a passage-fallback planner that falls back from graph to passage retrieval when graph candidates are missing/stale/contradicted/low-confidence, recorded with an explainable reason against PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority. The code implements the decision (passage_fallback.rs), wires it into one executed retrieval pipeline on real managed PostgreSQL (executor.rs, reachable from a backend API), and persists the fallback with a durable mode_reason and replayable trace. Real-PG integration tests prove missing/stale/no-result fallback end-to-end.
findings: passage_fallback.rs:87 decide_passage_fallback implements all four MT-named triggers with precedence contradiction>staleness>missing>low-confidence (lines 92-102), each mapped to an actor-visible reason string (as_str, lines 36-43); pure, deterministic, no escapes.

executor.rs:89 execute_retrieval is the single executed PG path (plan -> schema_filter -> graph traversal -> decide_passage_fallback line 153 -> rank -> snippet -> compile+persist); on fallback the plan is revised to PassageFallback (line 168), persisted mode column = hybrid_rag per MT-129 law, and the durable mode_reason + trace warnings/decisions carry the WHY (lines 160-173).
executor is wired into a real product surface, not just tests: api/knowledge_retrieval.rs:53,463 calls execute_retrieval; mod.rs:39 exports pub mod executor.

Real managed-PostgreSQL proof: tests/knowledge_memory_fixtures.rs setup uses knowledge_pg()/managed-PG (skip_if_no_pg gate); pool_for connects to a real schema_url. load_passages_for_workspace (knowledge_memory/passage.rs:32) issues real SQL against knowledge_memory_passages. Not implementer-authored mocks.

Integration tests prove the executed fallback end-to-end on real PG: knowledge_retrieval_pipeline_tests.rs:481 (empty graph -> graph_candidates_missing -> 2 passages ranked by confidence -> span-cited bundle persisted -> trace mode hybrid_rag with fallback WHY in mode_reason/warnings/decisions), :1102 stale (freshness_uncertain over present graph -> graph_candidates_stale), :1175 no-result recovery -> persisted bounded empty bundle.
Prior adversarial-v2 MEDIUM (decision never wired/executed/integration-tested) is genuinely fixed in commit 136c5e9a: the executor and the two executed_pipeline_* PG tests close it.

CONCERN (not a fail): executor.rs:145 hardcodes GraphCandidateSignals.any_contradicted=false, so the contradiction-triggered arm is unit-proven (passage_fallback.rs:178) but not exercised through the live PG pipeline; three of four trigger paths (missing/stale/low-confidence) are proven end-to-end. The contradiction arm is real, reachable, escape-free code, so this is a wiring-completeness gap, not placeholder/fake.
No deferred-live escapes in the MT-133 impl or executor (grep for todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/Unavailable/placeholder/pending MT- returned NONE).

Aside (non-blocking): packet cites spec v02.192 anchors; resolved against current v02.194 spec module 02-system-architecture.md 2.3.13.11 which aligns (MemoryPassage bounded context unit, explainable retrieval modes, durable PG/EventLedger authority).

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
